name: Third Secret Fatima
description: The Third Secret of Fatima is the final part of a series of apocalyptic prophecies and visions said to have been revealed by the Virgin Mary to three Portuguese shepherd children, Lúcia Santos and her cousins Jacinta and Francisco Marto, in 1917. The first two secrets, revealed earlier, concerned a vision of hell and a prediction of the end of World War I and the rise and fall of communism. The Third Secret was written down by Lúcia in 1944 and sealed in an envelope, to be opened by the Pope no later than 1960. It was finally revealed by the Vatican in 2000, describing a vision of a 'bishop dressed in white' being killed by soldiers amid the ruins of a city, interpreted as a prophecy of the assassination attempt on Pope John Paul II in 1981. However, the Vatican's interpretation has been met with skepticism and controversy. Many believe that the Vatican has not revealed the full secret, and that the actual content is far more disturbing, perhaps concerning a major apostasy within the Church, a nuclear war, or some other catastrophic event. Proponents of this view often cite inconsistencies in the Vatican's statements and the testimony of individuals close to the events. Mainstream scholarly perspective generally accepts the Vatican's interpretation, attributing alternative interpretations to religious fervor and a desire for sensationalism. Critics point to the lack of definitive evidence for a suppressed secret and argue that the published text aligns with the historical context of the 20th century, particularly the Cold War and the threat of nuclear annihilation. The ongoing debate reflects a tension between religious belief, historical interpretation, and the enduring power of prophecy.
subtitle: The final prophecy of Fatima.

claims.claim: The Vatican did not reveal the full Third Secret.
claims.evidence: Inconsistencies in Vatican statements, discrepancies in witness accounts, and the apocalyptic tone of the first two secrets suggest a more dire message.
claims.criticism: The Vatican maintains the released text is the complete Third Secret. Witness account discrepancies are common in eyewitness testimony, and the apocalyptic tone can be attributed to religious context.

claims.claim: The Third Secret predicts a major apostasy within the Catholic Church.
claims.evidence: Some interpret the 'bishop dressed in white' being killed as a symbol of the Church's internal corruption and decline in faith.
claims.criticism: The Vatican interprets the 'bishop' as Pope John Paul II, and the assassination attempt as a fulfillment of the prophecy, not an internal collapse.

claims.claim: The Third Secret warns of a future nuclear war or global catastrophe.
claims.evidence: The vision of a ruined city and widespread suffering is interpreted as a symbolic representation of a large-scale disaster.
claims.criticism: The Vatican interprets the vision as symbolic of the suffering of Christians in the 20th century, particularly during periods of persecution and war, but not necessarily predicting a specific event.

keyFigures.name: Lúcia Santos
keyFigures.role: One of the three seers of Fatima; wrote down the Third Secret.

keyFigures.name: Pope John Paul II
keyFigures.role: Whose assassination attempt was linked to the Third Secret; authorized its publication.

keyFigures.name: Antonio Socci
keyFigures.role: Italian journalist who argues the full secret was not revealed.

keyFigures.name: Cardinal Angelo Sodano
keyFigures.role: Vatican Secretary of State who presented the official interpretation of the Third Secret.

timeline.event: The apparitions of Our Lady of Fatima and the revelation of the three secrets.
Sister Lúcia writes down the Third Secret.
The year the Third Secret was supposed to be revealed according to Lúcia's instructions.
The assassination attempt on Pope John Paul II.
The Vatican publishes its interpretation of the Third Secret.

sources.author: Sandra Zimdars-Swartz
sources.work: Encountering Mary: From La Salette to Medjugorje
sources.title: Sandra Zimdars-Swartz
